title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Indigenous Americas origin
description: Taíno Caribbean — Columbus 1492 encounters 'cohiba' ceremonial tobacco. Nicotiana rustica cultivated in Americas 6,000+ years ago.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Indigenous Americas origin
description: Mesoamerica — Aztec + Maya ceremonial smoking; 'picietl' in Nahuatl.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Indigenous Americas origin
description: Chesapeake Bay (Powhatan) — tobacco is central to diplomacy; later 'saved' Jamestown economy.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Chesapeake colonial plantations
description: Virginia — John Rolfe plants Nicotiana tabacum 1612. Tobacco saves Jamestown from failure.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Chesapeake colonial plantations
description: North Carolina — tobacco belt built on enslaved African labour.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Chesapeake colonial plantations
description: London — colonial tobacco trade enriches British merchants; taxes fund British state.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Ottoman + Asian spread
description: Istanbul — Ottoman court adopts tobacco 1600s despite periodic bans. Hookah smoking develops.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Ottoman + Asian spread
description: Canton China — Portuguese introduce tobacco via Macau; Qing emperors alternately ban + tax.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Ottoman + Asian spread
description: India — Portuguese Jesuits introduce tobacco 1605; now India #2 world producer.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Modern industrial tobacco + Marlboro Man
description: Tennessee + Winston-Salem NC — R.J. Reynolds + Philip Morris build Big Tobacco industry.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Modern industrial tobacco + Marlboro Man
description: Yunnan China — China National Tobacco Corporation state monopoly, now the world's largest cigarette maker.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: 'tobacco' etymology
description: Taíno — 'tabaco' (may have originally meant the rolled smoking tube, not the plant).

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: 'tobacco' etymology
description: Spanish — 'tabaco'. Preserves the Taíno word as the global root.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: 'tobacco' etymology
description: English — 'tobacco'. Nearly all European languages retain the Taíno root.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: China — 43% of world tobacco production, near-total state monopoly.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: India — 9% of world production, Andhra Pradesh + Gujarat.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Brazil — 11% of world production, major exporter.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Malawi + Zimbabwe + Zambia — African tobacco belt, significant exporter to global manufacturers.
